message:
docs(fnx-algorithms): rename misleading lr_planarity_test, document gap

The function previously named lr_planarity_test() did NOT implement
Left-Right planarity testing — its lowpoint computation was unused and
its only real check was the Euler edge bound |E| <= 3|V| - 6 applied
globally and per biconnected component. That check is necessary but
not sufficient: K3,3 (n=6, m=9) and the Petersen graph (n=10, m=15)
both satisfy the bound yet are non-planar, and the function reported
both as planar.

Renames the function to planar_edge_bound_with_bcc to match its actual
behavior, and adds a comment on is_planar() pointing readers at the
known correctness gap (br-isplanarbroken: Python wrapper currently
routes around this via check_planarity → nx). No behavior change; this
is purely a code-honesty pass to make the misleading name stop lying.

Bead for the underlying correctness gap will be filed once the bead DB
lock contention clears.
